text.heading: Suspected kidnappers of Ondo officials’ wives arrested
text.p: The efforts of the Ondo State Police Command to secure the release of the wives of the Commissioner for Lands and Housing, and a Permanent Secretary, Mrs Iyabo Basaru and Mrs Sola Alli, who were abducted last Sunday, paid off yesterday with the arrest of the suspected kidnappers.
The suspects were arrested by policemen after they were tricked that they would be paid a ransom to secure the release of the hostages.
Earlier, Mrs Alli was said to have escaped from the hands of her abductors and made her way home yesterday.
Speaking with reporters yesterday in Akure, Commissioner of Police Charles Dawodu said the women were released after a gun duel with the hoodlums.
He was, however, silent on whether a ransom was paid to the kidnappers before they were apprehended.
But a source told The Nation that a N10million ransom was paid before the breakthrough was recorded.

There was jubilation at Ajipowo and Alagbaka Government Reservation Area (GRA), Akure home of the freed hostages. Family members, friends and well – wishers stormed the Basarus’ and Allis’ homes to rejoice with them.
A source said the suspected kidnappers were arrested at their hideout in Edo State.
One of them, whose identity was still been shielded, was killed in the gun duel, it was learnt.
Dawodu, who refused to give further details on the arrests, said the suspects were in the custody of the police.
Assistant-Inspector General of Police (AIG) in charge of Zone II, Mr. Mohammad Yesufu, flew into the state following the news of the kidnapping on Sunday, to join in the manhunt for the kidnappers.
An elated Governor Olusegun Mimiko congratulated the police, the State Security Service (SSS) and all those involved in the foiling of the crime.
Mimiko, in a release issued by his Chief Press Secretary (CPS) Kolawole Olabisi in Akure, yesterday, hailed the courage, diligence and commitment of the officers.

The statement reads in part: "We congratulate the two families and urge them to move on with life, unencumbered by the psychological trauma that may have been caused by the unholy kidnap. As a government, we have stated that we shall not submit to blackmail of any kind and will strive at all times to protect the interest of the larger majority of people who are law-abiding and peace-loving.

"We cannot afford to surrender to a few who have taken the alternative of seeking wealth and recognition to the detriment of the society, hence our refusal to negotiate with the kidnappers till the release of our dear citizens were secured.

"We salute their families for calmly bearing the burden of missing their loved ones while their fate appeared uncertain. Our faith in God, like theirs was unshaken that the ungodly effort will be thwarted.

"We are full of thanks to God for answering our prayers by rewarding the diligence of the security agencies. Once again, government congratulates the security agencies for a job well done and rejoices with the families on a sorrow-free re-union."
